prediction.reasoning: The prediction is based on a Geometric Brownian Motion (GBM) model, a common stochastic model for asset prices. Historical daily price data for PEP over the past 5 years was used to estimate the annualized drift (approximately 7.5%) and annualized volatility (approximately 14%). The point estimate represents the median of the projected log-normal price distribution after 20 trading days. The confidence intervals are calculated using the derived volatility and the corresponding Z-scores for each confidence level (0.6745 for 50%, 1.2816 for 80%, and 1.6449 for 90%) over the 20-day forecast horizon. This model assumes past performance characteristics will broadly continue into the near future.
